summary: Deterministic prompt pack for a GitHub Copilot coding agent to build a new Streamlit Digital Ecosystem Platform app with UI parity to adaptive-sales-engine, AI agent taxonomy aligned with AI-FACTORY-v2, multi-company selection, and explicit industrial IIoT + AI-agent capabilities.
steps: 1. Input grounding: Confirm references and lock scope to (a) visual/UI concept from adaptive-sales-engine, (b) settings + AI agent taxonomy from AI-FACTORY-v2, and (c) new Streamlit app purpose: Digital Ecosystem Platform for machine manufacturers with multi-company workspace selection.

2. Requirement extraction: Convert the long-form brief into immutable requirement groups: baseline IIoT features, current limitations, AI-native target architecture, agent roles, autonomy progression, after-sales growth levers, and required literal constraints.

3. Build instruction prompt: Generate a single implementation instruction for Copilot Agent that includes project objective, architecture layers, functional modules, UX behavior, data and orchestration expectations, and non-functional constraints.
4. Enforce deterministic output contract: Require fixed section order, explicit acceptance criteria, no omitted constraints, and unambiguous implementation directives.
5. Add validation checks: Verify that output includes all mandatory blueprint blocks, maturity model mapping (L1-L5), after-sales monetization logic, and explicit phrases required by constraints.
6. Critique loop pass 1: Identify ambiguity, missing constraints, weak actionability, and non-testable language. Produce corrections.
7. Critique loop pass 2: Tighten wording for low-iteration execution, remove narrative drift, and ensure every module has measurable expected behavior.
8. Finalize reusable prompt: Emit final instruction prompt plus refinement history and a completion checklist so future tasks can reuse the same structure.
constraints: Must be based on repository reference: C:\Users\Inaki Senar\Documents\GitHub\adaptive-sales-engine
Must implement setting and AI agent taxonomy from: C:\Users\Inaki Senar\Documents\GitHub\AI-FACTORY-v2
Must instruct creation of a new Streamlit application with the same visual and UI concept as adaptive-sales-engine
Must support working with many different companies by selecting the target company context
Must include the complete BEST Digital Ecosystem blueprint (Section 6 from brief)
Must include maturity model guidance (Section 8 from brief)
Must preserve and include the literal constraint fragment: 'Without it:'
Must preserve and include the literal constraint fragment: 'when upgrades are needed'
Must preserve and include the literal constraint fragment: '○ "You will need this part in 3 weeks"'
Must preserve and include the literal constraint fragment: 'You need:'
Must remain unambiguous, actionable, and deterministic for LLM execution
Must return strict JSON output with keys: summary, steps, constraints, output_schema, quality_gates, final_answer

summary.description: One concise objective statement of the generated prompt pack.

steps.description: Numbered execution steps for the generation workflow.

constraints.description: Hard constraints that must be fully preserved.

output_schema.description: Schema contract for deterministic response structure.

quality_gates.description: Pass/fail checks before accepting final prompt output.

quality_gates: Gate 1: Output is valid JSON and parseable without modification.
Gate 2: Top-level keys exactly include summary, steps, constraints, output_schema, quality_gates, final_answer.
Gate 3: Steps are numbered and execution-ready with no ambiguous verbs.
Gate 4: All required constraints from the brief are explicitly present, including literal fragments.
Gate 5: Prompt includes Section 6 blueprint content and Section 8 maturity model mapping.

Gate 6: Prompt includes after-sales service growth keys (installed base visibility, predictive/prescriptive service, productized services, recurring revenue, upsell triggers, parts optimization, interface ownership, AI agents, value demonstration, lifecycle strategy, internal alignment).
Gate 7: Critique refinement history has at least 2 iterations and each includes issue + fix pairs.
Gate 8: Final instruction is directly executable by GitHub Copilot agent in a low-iteration workflow.

final_answer.copilot_instruction_prompt:
You are GitHub Copilot Agent. Build a NEW Streamlit application for a Digital Ecosystem Platform for machine manufacturers. Mirror the visual and UI concept from C:\Users\Inaki Senar\Documents\GitHub\adaptive-sales-engine and implement setting logic + AI agent taxonomy from C:\Users\Inaki Senar\Documents\GitHub\AI-FACTORY-v2.

Execution requirements (deterministic):
1) App framing and tenancy
- Build multi-company workspace selection at entry (company picker).
- Persist selected company as active context for all modules.
- Show clear active-company indicator in UI header/sidebar.

2) Baseline IIoT platform capabilities (market-proven)
- Include modules for machine connectivity, real-time ingestion/analytics, predictive maintenance, remote monitoring/diagnostics, ERP/MES integration hooks, digital twin feedback loop, and API/app extensibility framing.

3) Limitation-to-innovation framing
- Represent transition from data-centric dashboards to action-centric intelligence workflows.
- Encode flow shift: Today = Data -> Dashboard -> Human -> Decision -> Action; Future = Data -> AI Agents -> Decision -> Action (+ optional human approval).

4) Target architecture implementation
- Layer 1 (Physical/Data): machines, PLCs, sensors, edge, cloud ingestion connectors.
- Layer 2 (Digital Core): streaming + data lake + digital twins (machine/process/plant/supply chain).
- Layer 3 (AI Agent Layer): orchestration and specialized agents below.

5) Mandatory AI agents
- Operational Agent: anomaly detection + corrective action proposals/execution.
- Optimization Agent: energy/material/throughput optimization with scenario simulation.
- Maintenance Agent: failure prediction + intervention scheduling + spare-part ordering.
- Commercial Agent: upsell/cross-sell prompts based on usage.
- Engineering Agent: feed field performance into product/design improvement loop.
- Management/CEO Agent: strategic synthesis, risk, investment recommendations.
- Customer Success Agent: conversational advisory for plant performance improvement.

6) BEST Digital Ecosystem blueprint (must be explicit)
- Hyper-connectivity (OEM-agnostic + legacy compatibility).
- Digital twin of machines/processes/plants/supply chain/customer usage.
- AI Agent Orchestration Engine (priority/conflict resolution).
- Autonomous execution layer (PLC/MES/ERP actions).
- App + Agent Marketplace concept.
- Continuous learning loop and anonymized cross-customer benchmarking.
- Embedded commercial layer (spares ecommerce, subscriptions, performance-based pricing).
- Human-AI interface (conversational + explainability).

7) Maturity model support (L1-L5)
- Implement maturity indicator and state descriptions: L1 Monitoring, L2 Analytics, L3 Predictive, L4 Semi-autonomous, L5 Fully autonomous AI ecosystem.
- Provide progression recommendations to move customers from current level to next level.

8) After-sales growth engine modules
- Installed base visibility registry and segmentation.
- Reactive -> Predictive -> Prescriptive service workflow.
- Productized service catalog (outcome-based offers).
- Recurring revenue tiers (Basic/Advanced/Premium).
- Data-driven triggered upsell logic, including: when upgrades are needed and "You will need this part in 3 weeks".
- Spare parts optimization and automated replenishment.
- Remote diagnostics and support scalability.
- Customer interface ownership mechanics.
- AI revenue multiplier agents for opportunities, renewals, pricing, value proof.
- Lifecycle monetization journey and internal alignment hooks.

9) Critical constraints to keep verbatim in generated spec output
- Include phrase: Without it:
- Include phrase: when upgrades are needed
- Include phrase: "You will need this part in 3 weeks"
- Include phrase: You need:

10) Output requirements
- Return implementation plan with: architecture, UI map, data contracts, agent contracts, orchestration rules, acceptance criteria, and phased roadmap (MVP -> advanced).
- Keep language action-oriented and testable. No vague narrative-only text.

issues_found: Original draft mixed strategy narrative and build instructions, reducing execution clarity.
Missing explicit requirement to preserve literal constraint fragments.
No deterministic output contract for Copilot response.
improvements_applied: Rewrote as imperative build specification with numbered sections.
Added explicit output package requirements and acceptance-focused language.

issues_found: Architecture and agent taxonomy were present but after-sales monetization mechanics were incomplete.
Maturity model referenced but not operationalized.
Ambiguity remained around multi-company context persistence.
improvements_applied: Expanded after-sales module list with concrete service and revenue mechanisms.
Added maturity level indicator with progression recommendations.
Added explicit tenant/company selection persistence and UI visibility requirements.

reuse_instructions: Reuse by replacing only reference repository paths and domain-specific agent types.
Keep top-level JSON keys and gate structure unchanged for deterministic automation.
Run quality gates in order; reject output if any gate fails.
For future variants, append critique iterations instead of rewriting prior history.
Preserve literal constraints exactly when provided by stakeholders.
